So Long.info: A 2D game developed in C as part of the 42 curriculum. Built with MiniLibX, it features real-time rendering, collision detection, sprite animation, and a complete game loop.

Cube.info: A custom multithreaded raycasting game engine written in C. Originally developed for the 42 Cub3D project, it was expanded with vertical camera and world positioning, procedural map generation, dynamic lighting, post-processing effects, configurable rendering options, advanced minimaps, and a Pokémon-inspired RPG gameplay system complete with NPCs, battles, and collectibles.

Ascii Run.info: A side-scrolling platformer rendered entirely with ASCII characters inside the terminal. Built on top of my custom ASCII rendering engine, featuring real-time animation, physics, and keyboard input.

Ascii Fight.info: A top-down action RPG rendered exclusively in the terminal using ASCII graphics. Features real-time combat, animated entities, and a custom rendering engine written from scratch.

Ascii Jump.info: A vertical platformer displayed entirely in the terminal. Demonstrates procedural level generation, and smooth ASCII rendering at interactive frame rates.

Ascii Space.info: A terminal-based space shooter powered by my custom ASCII rendering engine. Includes real-time movement, particle effects, enemy AI, and responsive gameplay entirely within a console.

Philosophers.info: An enhanced implementation of the classic Dining Philosophers concurrency problem. Beyond multithreading and synchronization, it includes a live ASCII visualization that displays the state of every philosopher in real time.

Sudoku.info: A complete Sudoku game developed in Unity with C#. Features puzzle generation, validation logic, interactive gameplay, and a polished user interface.

Chess.info: A fully playable chess game developed in Unity. Implements legal move generation, game state management, captures, special moves, and a clean interactive interface.
